# Community Proof Statistics

<!-- generated by tools/leaderboard/generate.py — do not edit by hand -->

Verified output comes from active `library/index` records plus archived index records only after their active copy has been retired; append-only terminal-run telemetry comes from `proof-runs/`. Rates cover only logged runs and never guess historical failures from Git history. Timing is contributor-reported local proof plus verification time.

**1404 verified proofs · 1096 attributed · 308 historical/unknown · 476 logged terminal runs.**

## Contributor Leaderboard

Rank uses Score (difficulty points + dispatch credit). Explicit `solver≜...` provenance wins; older proof records without solver provenance use git add-author attribution as inferred historical credit. **Dispatch credit** awards 0.9 points to the contributor who opened/landed someone else's proof PR (self-dispatch excluded); it is added to Score.

| Rank | Contributor | Proof credit | Explicit | Inferred | Runs | Run success | Difficulty points | Dispatch (0.9 ea) | Score |

## Attribution Notes

**1096 explicit solver credits · 308 inferred git credits · 0 uncredited proof records.**

Git add-author attribution covers 1404 of 1404 proof index files. It is used only where explicit `solver≜` provenance is missing.

## Interpretation

- Leaderboard rank is based on verified proofs, then difficulty points; failures are credited as effort but do not improve rank.
- Run and attempt rates are descriptive, not causal model comparisons. Stratify by difficulty and wait for useful sample sizes.
- Infrastructure failures are excluded because they provide no evidence about mathematical or model performance.
- Historical proofs and failed attempts before this telemetry existed remain unknown rather than reconstructed.
